evidence_quotes.quote: For video games, developers should still have to leave them playable and remain in my possession after the end of the developers’ official support.
With regard to the playability of multi-players, it should be mandatory for developers to be able to use community servers or play via peer to peer.
There should be no online coercion for single players and thus the game should remain playable.
multiplayers can continue to play and single players cannot be switched off at all

On the topic of digital media acquisition, if the distribution platform is discontinued, I should be able to download it as a file and thus manage it on a private memory in order to be able to continue using it.

paragraphs.text: Dear, I would like to provide you with feedback on the Digital Fairness Act. I would like to ask you to add consumer protection to digitally purchased media in addition to your above-mentioned points. This should include video games, films, music, e-books and the like. For video games, developers should still have to leave them playable and remain in my possession after the end of the developers’ official support. With regard to the playability of multi-players, it should be mandatory for developers to be able to use community servers or play via peer to peer. There should be no online coercion for single players and thus the game should remain playable. On the topic of digital media acquisition, if the distribution platform is discontinued, I should be able to download it as a file and thus manage it on a private memory in order to be able to continue using it. I am worried that we are moving forward in a future where, as a citizen, we will no longer have anything and must be satisfied with it. I do not want to see this future, and I would therefore ask you to improve consumer protection in digital media. I want to be digital media as a perpetual form of entertainment and as part of our history, and this is not possible if developers and sellers deprive us of this opportunity and citizens can sue and stop them. Examples in the area of video games for games that are no longer playable are recent examples: The crew of Ubisoft and Concord of Sony Stands has been announced by EA that Anthem will soon become unplayable. Therefore, my appeal to you is that developers should be obliged to give the community the possibility that multiplayers can continue to play and single players cannot be switched off at all, and online traders should not be able to withdraw my acquired digital media or, at the end of their service, allow them to download and store them locally. Thank you for your efforts and I hope that you will consider my feedback. Yours sincerely, Jannik Heitmann
